 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p03m","title":"Add DCG user experience improvements","description":"## Task: Add DCG user experience improvements

### What to Do

Ensure the DCG integration provides excellent user experience across all touchpoints.

### UX Checkpoints

#### 1. Installation Feedback
- [ ] Clear "Installing DCG..." message during install
- [ ] Success message with `dcg doctor` suggestion
- [ ] Failure message with recovery steps
- [ ] Hook registration confirmation

#### 2. First-Time User Experience
In services-setup.sh TUI:
- [ ] Brief DCG explanation before first use
- [ ] Show example denial with explanation
- [ ] Teach `dcg test` command
- [ ] Explain allow-once workflow

#### 3. Error Message Quality
All DCG-related error messages should:
- [ ] Explain WHAT happened clearly
- [ ] Explain WHY it happened
- [ ] Suggest NEXT STEPS
- [ ] Provide HELP COMMAND (`dcg doctor`, `dcg --help`)

#### 4. Website Discoverability
- [ ] DCG visible "above the fold" on landing page
- [ ] DCG in main navigation or tools dropdown
- [ ] Search indexes DCG content
- [ ] Cross-links between DCG pages

#### 5. Recovery Experience
When things go wrong:
- [ ] `dcg doctor` provides actionable fixes
- [ ] Uninstall instructions easy to find
- [ ] Reinstall path is simple
- [ ] Support/issues link provided

### Implementation Details

#### Add helpful messages to installer:
```bash
log_info "Installing DCG (Destructive Command Guard)..."
log_info "DCG will protect your codebase from dangerous commands"
# ... installation ...
log_info "DCG installed! Run 'dcg doctor' to verify installation"
```

#### Add TUI onboarding card:
```bash
# In services-setup.sh
show_dcg_onboarding() {
    gum style \
        --border normal \
        --margin "1" \
        --padding "1" \
        --border-foreground 212 \
        "DCG (Destructive Command Guard) is now active!

It will block dangerous commands like:
  • git reset --hard
  • rm -rf ./src
  • DROP TABLE users

If blocked, you'll see a denial message with:
  • The pattern that matched
  • A safer alternative
  • A short code for legitimate bypass

Try it: dcg test 'git status'  (should allow)
Try it: dcg test 'git reset --hard'  (should deny)"
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New users understand DCG within 30 seconds
- [ ] Error messages always include next steps
- [ ] Recovery from any DCG issue takes < 5 minutes
- [ ] DCG discoverable within 2 clicks from homepage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:20:09.460572503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:49:13.208901321Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:49:13.208901321Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p03m","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-buxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:20:19.389858409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p03m","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-oggw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:20:20.799144493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p03m","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-z7om","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:20:18.078674259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p150","title":"SRPS: Add to lessons.ts - Lesson Metadata","description":"# Task: Add SRPS to /apps/web/lib/lessons.ts

## What This File Does

`lessons.ts` contains the LESSONS array - metadata for each tutorial in the Learning Hub.
This is DIFFERENT from `lessons/index.tsx` (which handles rendering).

Each entry contains:
- `id`: Sequential number (0-indexed)
- `slug`: URL path segment (e.g., "srps" → /learn/srps)
- `title`: Display title
- `description`: Brief description
- `duration`: Estimated reading time
- `file`: Source markdown file name (if using markdown-based lesson)

## What to Add

Add SRPS entry at the end of the LESSONS array:

```typescript
{
  id: 23,  // Next sequential ID after current last entry (22 for meta-skill)
  slug: "srps",
  title: "SRPS: System Protection",
  description: "Keep your workstation responsive under heavy agent load",
  duration: "8 min",
  file: "23_srps.md",  // Markdown file for structured lesson OR use component
},
```

## Location in File

Find `export const LESSONS: Lesson[] = [` and add to the end of the array,
just before the closing `];`.

## Important Considerations

### Option A: Markdown-Based Lesson (Recommended Pattern)
If following the existing pattern, create a corresponding markdown file:
- Location: `/acfs/onboard/lessons/23_srps.md`
- Format: Standard lesson markdown with sections, code blocks, etc.

### Option B: Component-Only Lesson
If using pure React component (srps-lesson.tsx):
- The `file` field can be omitted or set to empty string
- The lesson routing will use the component instead of markdown

**Recommendation**: Follow Option A for consistency with existing lessons.
This means also creating the markdown file (separate task).

## Validation

1. `cd apps/web && bun run build` - TypeScript must compile
2. Import and call `getLessonBySlug("srps")` - Should return the lesson object
3. Check `/learn/srps` renders correctly
4. Verify lesson appears in learning path navigation

## Integration Notes

- The `id` must be sequential (check current max ID in file)
- The `slug` must match what's used in:
  - `lessons/index.tsx` switch case
  - `tool-data.tsx` related lessons
  - Any URLs/links to the lesson

## Lesson Interface Reference

```typescript
export interface Lesson {
  id: number;        // Sequential 0-indexed
  slug: string;      // URL segment
  title: string;     // Display title
  description: string;
  duration: string;  // e.g., "8 min"
  file: string;      // Markdown filename
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T06:04:12.604652900Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:16:26.827330213Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:16:26.827276402Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added SRPS to lessons.ts as ID 23 and created 23_srps.md lesson file. Build validates successfully with 21 learn paths.","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p1w8","title":"Add dcg to TOOL_TERMS in glossary page","description":"## Task: Add dcg to TOOL_TERMS in glossary page

### What to Do

Add "dcg" to the TOOL_TERMS set so it's categorized as a tool in the glossary.

### Location

File: /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/app/learn/glossary/page.tsx
Line: ~20 (TOOL_TERMS constant)

### Current Code

```typescript
const TOOL_TERMS = new Set([
  "tmux", "zsh", "bash", "bun", "uv", "cargo", "rust", "go", "git", "gh",
  "lazygit", "rg", "ripgrep", "fzf", "direnv", "zoxide", "atuin", "ntm",
  "bv", "bd", "ubs", "cass", "cm", "caam", "slb", "vault", "wrangler",
  "supabase", "vercel", "postgres",
]);
```

### New Code

```typescript
const TOOL_TERMS = new Set([
  "tmux", "zsh", "bash", "bun", "uv", "cargo", "rust", "go", "git", "gh",
  "lazygit", "rg", "ripgrep", "fzf", "direnv", "zoxide", "atuin", "ntm",
  "bv", "bd", "ubs", "cass", "cm", "caam", "slb", "dcg", "vault", "wrangler",
  "supabase", "vercel", "postgres",
]);
```

### Rationale

The TOOL_TERMS set is used by the categorizeTerm() function to determine how to display terms. Without "dcg" in this set, DCG would be miscategorized as a concept or acronym.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:51:00.860455303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:16:06.183448489Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:16:06.183448489Z","close_reason":"Committed in 26ca3d2","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p1w8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-xbun","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:53:12.767758733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-p4qw","title":"EPIC: apr (Automated Plan Reviser Pro) Full ACFS Integration","description":"# EPIC: apr (Automated Plan Reviser Pro) Full ACFS Integration

## Background & Context
apr is a Bash-based iterative specification refinement tool that uses GPT Pro 5.2 Extended Reasoning via Oracle. It helps developers create comprehensive, well-structured project specifications through an interactive refinement loop.

## Technical Specifications
- **Binary**: `apr`
- **Language**: Bash script
- **Install Method**: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash`
- **Config Location**: `.apr/` per-project directory
- **Verification**: `apr --version`
- **Update**: `apr update` or re-run installer
- **Robot Mode**: `apr robot <cmd>` for JSON API output
- **Dependencies**: Oracle CLI (for GPT Pro 5.2 access), gum (for TUI)

## Classification
**CORE STACK MEMBER** - Essential planning tool for the Dicklesworthstone workflow.

## Integration Scope
This EPIC covers full integration into ACFS including:
1. Manifest entry in acfs.manifest.yaml (phase: stack, category: tools)
2. Checksums.yaml entry for verified installer
3. Doctor command health checks (verify apr + Oracle connectivity)
4. Update command support
5. Webapp/wizard mentions
6. Learning Hub lesson (spec refinement workflow)
7. Onboarding TUI lesson
8. TLDR/Command Reference entry

## Dependencies & Relationships
- Depends on: Oracle CLI, gum TUI library
- Works with: Project planning workflows, spec-driven development
- Complements: jfp (prompts) and ms (skills)

## Success Criteria
- `acfs doctor` shows apr status and Oracle connectivity
- `acfs update` can update apr
- Learning Hub explains apr workflow with examples
- Onboarding shows apr's role in spec-driven development","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:18:31.229364127Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T18:18:31.229364127Z","compaction_level":0,"labels":["apr","epic","stack-tool"]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pdxz","title":"[Phase 8] DCG Website Lessons System Integration","description":"## Phase 8: Website Lessons System Integration

Create a dedicated DCG lesson and integrate DCG into the existing safety-tools lesson.

### Background

The ACFS learning hub has lessons for each major tool (NTM, UBS, CASS, etc.). Each lesson has:
- A React component (apps/web/components/lessons/X-lesson.tsx)
- An entry in lessons.ts (defines slug, title, description, duration)
- Registration in the index.tsx switch statement

Currently, DCG has no dedicated lesson, and the safety-tools-lesson only covers SLB and CAAM.

### Why This Matters

Lessons are how users learn to use tools effectively. Without a DCG lesson, users won't understand:
- What DCG protects against
- How to test commands before running
- How to handle blocked commands
- How to configure packs

### Implementation Details

1. Create dcg-lesson.tsx component
2. Add DCG lesson entry to lessons.ts
3. Register DCG in lessons/index.tsx
4. Update safety-tools-lesson.tsx to include DCG

### Files to Create

- apps/web/components/lessons/dcg-lesson.tsx

### Files to Modify

- apps/web/lib/lessons.ts: Add DCG lesson entry
- apps/web/components/lessons/index.tsx: Register DCG lesson
- apps/web/components/lessons/safety-tools-lesson.tsx: Add DCG section

### Acceptance Criteria

- /learn/dcg route works and shows DCG lesson
- Lesson appears in learning hub navigation
- Safety tools lesson mentions DCG alongside SLB/CAAM","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:49:49.173506194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:21:51.803171831Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:21:51.803171831Z","close_reason":"Added dcg-lesson component, wired slug in lessons index, and expanded safety-tools lesson to include DCG","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pdxz","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7ifk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:52:40.400624971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
